title: AI calorie burn tracking from workouts and step count via device physical activity permission

requirements.summary: Extend WorkoutLogForm to include a duration field in minutes and store it with workout logs
acceptanceCriteria: WorkoutLogForm renders a numeric 'Duration (minutes)' input field
The duration value is submitted to the backend with the workout log entry
Backend Workout type includes a duration field (Nat) and stores it correctly

file_operations.description: Add a numeric duration input field (in minutes) to the workout form alongside muscle group, exercise, sets, reps, weight, and date. Include validation to ensure duration is a positive number. Update the form submission to include the duration value when calling the logWorkout mutation.

requirements.summary: Implement AI-based calorie burn estimation using workout data and user body weight, displaying per-workout and daily/weekly totals
acceptanceCriteria: Each workout entry shows an AI-calculated estimated calories burned value based on exercise type and duration
The calculation uses the user's body weight when available to improve accuracy (MET-based or heuristic formula)
A total estimated calories burned for the day or week is visible in the fitness section
The calorie burn estimate updates when new workouts are logged

file_operations.description: Create a utility module that calculates estimated calories burned using a MET-based formula. Export a function that takes exercise type (string), duration (minutes), and optional body weight (kg) as parameters. Implement a lookup table mapping common exercise names and muscle groups to MET values (Metabolic Equivalent of Task). Use the formula: calories = MET × body weight (kg) × duration (hours). Default to 70kg if body weight is unavailable. Return the estimated calories as a number.

Import the calorie calculator utility from frontend/src/utils/calorieCalculator.ts. For each workout in the workouts array, calculate estimated calories burned using the workout's exercise, duration, and the user's body weight from their profile. Display the estimated calories burned per workout session in the AI suggestions. Calculate and display a total estimated calories burned for the current day or week. Update the suggestions to reflect the new calorie burn insights when workouts are logged.

requirements.summary: Add step tracking feature using device motion/accelerometer APIs with permission request, displaying live step count and allowing save to backend
acceptanceCriteria: On first use, the app requests physical activity / accelerometer permission from the browser or device
If permission is granted, the app counts steps in real time using device motion/accelerometer data
The current step count is displayed prominently on the fitness dashboard
The user can save the tracked step count to their daily metrics in the backend
If permission is denied, the app gracefully falls back to the manual step entry in DailyMetricsForm with a clear message explaining why automatic tracking is unavailable

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that manages step tracking using the DeviceMotionEvent or Accelerometer API. Request permission via navigator.permissions.query for 'accelerometer' or by attaching a DeviceMotionEvent listener. Track accelerometer data to detect steps using a step detection algorithm (e.g., peak detection on acceleration magnitude). Maintain step count state and expose functions to start tracking, stop tracking, reset count, and check permission status. Return the current step count, permission status ('granted', 'denied', 'prompt', or 'unsupported'), and control functions. Handle permission denial gracefully and provide an error message state.

Create a widget component that uses the useStepTracker hook from frontend/src/hooks/useStepTracker.ts. Display the current step count prominently with a start/stop tracking button. If permission is 'prompt' or 'unsupported', show a button to request permission with explanatory text. If permission is 'denied', display a message explaining that automatic tracking is unavailable and link to the manual step entry in DailyMetricsForm. Provide a 'Save Steps' button that pre-fills the DailyMetricsForm with the tracked step count and today's date, opening the daily metrics dialog. Style the widget with fitness accent colors matching the existing design system.

Import and render the StepTrackerWidget component from frontend/src/components/StepTrackerWidget.tsx near the top of the fitness dashboard, above or alongside the stats summary. Ensure the widget is visible and accessible for users to start/stop tracking and save their step count. Wire the widget's 'Save Steps' action to open the existing daily metrics dialog with pre-filled step count.

Add support for pre-filling the steps field from an external source (e.g., StepTrackerWidget). Accept optional initialSteps and initialDate props. If provided, populate the steps input and date picker with these values when the form mounts. Maintain existing functionality for manual step entry and validation.

requirements.summary: Update FitnessSection stats summary to include a 'Calories Burned' stat card derived from AI-estimated workout calorie burns
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Calories Burned' stat card is visible in the FitnessSection stats area
The value reflects the sum of AI-estimated calories burned from all logged workouts for the current day or selected period
The card updates reactively when new workouts are added

file_operations.description: Import the calorie calculator utility from frontend/src/utils/calorieCalculator.ts. In the stats summary section, calculate the total estimated calories burned by summing the calorie estimates for all workouts (using each workout's exercise, duration, and the user's body weight from their profile). Add a new stat card titled 'Calories Burned' displaying this total, styled consistently with the existing workout, steps, and calorie intake stat cards. Use the fitness accent color (green) for the icon. Ensure the card updates reactively when the workouts query data changes.
